interaction.prompt: Offensive-zone faceoff, weak side. You're the strong-side winger. Tap where you should set up before the draw.

feedback.right: Strong-side wall — you're the safety valve if the C wins it back, and you're high enough to support the breakout if the puck pops out.
feedback.wrong: Don't crowd the dot. Strong-side wing's job is to hold the wall — high enough to support, low enough to win a battle.

tip: OZ-faceoff weak side: strong-side wing OWNS the strong-side wall. Don't drift inside; hold position.
why: Faceoff positioning teaches role discipline. The wall is yours; the slot belongs to the other winger; the points are the D's. Don't poach.
